nodes.name: Issues-Kaggle-Notebooks
aliases: Jupyter and Kaggle notebooks, GitHub issues

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Pretraining (Stages 1 and 2) — FineWeb-Edu was used as part of the 85% web mix (12% multilingual) in the staged 11.2T-token curriculum.

evidence.location: Pretraining section, three-stage data mix bullet
evidence.excerpt: Web: 85% (12% multilingual) - FineWeb-Edu, DCLM, FineWeb2 and FineWeb2-HQ
evidence.explanation: Names FineWeb-Edu among the four web sources composing 85% of the pretraining mix.

evidence.excerpt: HuggingFaceFW/fineweb-edu
evidence.explanation: Authoritative SmolLM3 pretraining dataset collection lists FineWeb-Edu by HF repo path.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Pretraining web component — DCLM-baseline-1.0 was one of four web datasets composing the 85% web slice across Stages 1 and 2.

evidence.location: Pretraining section, three-stage data mix bullet
evidence.excerpt: Web: 85% (12% multilingual) - FineWeb-Edu, DCLM, FineWeb2 and FineWeb2-HQ
evidence.explanation: Lists DCLM as a constituent web source of the pretraining mix.

evidence.excerpt: mlfoundations/dclm-baseline-1.0
evidence.explanation: Resolves DCLM to its authoritative HF release.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Pretraining multilingual web component — FineWeb2 contributed to the 12% multilingual portion of the 85% web slice.

evidence.location: Pretraining section, three-stage data mix bullet
evidence.excerpt: Web: 85% (12% multilingual) - FineWeb-Edu, DCLM, FineWeb2 and FineWeb2-HQ
evidence.explanation: Names FineWeb2 among the four web sources of the pretraining mix.

evidence.excerpt: HuggingFaceFW/fineweb-2
evidence.explanation: Resolves FineWeb2 to its authoritative HF release.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Pretraining high-quality multilingual web component contributing to the 12% multilingual portion.

evidence.location: Pretraining section, three-stage data mix bullet
evidence.excerpt: Web: 85% (12% multilingual) - FineWeb-Edu, DCLM, FineWeb2 and FineWeb2-HQ
evidence.explanation: Lists FineWeb2-HQ as a web source.

evidence.excerpt: epfml/FineWeb2-HQ
evidence.explanation: Resolves FineWeb2-HQ to its authoritative EPFL HF release.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Math pretraining — Stage 1 used FineMath3+ (3% math slice) and Stage 2 used FineMath4+ (within 10% math slice). Both are released under HuggingFaceTB/finemath which also hosts the InfiMM-WebMath-3+/4+ filtered subsets.

evidence.location: Pretraining section, three-stage data mix bullet
evidence.excerpt: Math: 3% - FineMath3+ and InfiWebMath3+ ... Math: 10% - Introducing FineMath4+, InfiWebMath4+, and MegaMath
evidence.explanation: Names FineMath3+ and FineMath4+ as math pretraining sources.

evidence.excerpt: FineMath consists of 34B tokens (FineMath-3+) and 54B tokens (FineMath-3+ with InfiMM-WebMath-3+) of mathematical educational content filtered from CommonCrawl
evidence.explanation: Confirms FineMath3+/4+ and InfiWebMath3+/4+ are released under the HuggingFaceTB/finemath repository.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Pretraining code component — The Stack v2 (16 programming languages), StarCoder2 pull requests, and adjacent code corpora supplied the 12% code slice in Stage 1.

evidence.location: Pretraining section, three-stage data mix bullet
evidence.excerpt: Code: 12% - The Stack v2 (16 programming languages), StarCoder2 pull requests, Jupyter and Kaggle notebooks, GitHub issues, and StackExchange
evidence.explanation: Names The Stack v2 as the primary code pretraining source.

evidence.excerpt: bigcode/the-stack-v2
evidence.explanation: Resolves The Stack v2 to its BigCode HF release.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Pretraining code component supplying GitHub issues, Jupyter and Kaggle notebooks for the code slice.

evidence.location: Pretraining section, three-stage data mix bullet
evidence.excerpt: Jupyter and Kaggle notebooks, GitHub issues, and StackExchange
evidence.explanation: Names the Jupyter/Kaggle/issues components of the code mix.

evidence.excerpt: HuggingFaceTB/issues-kaggle-notebooks
evidence.explanation: Resolves the issues+kaggle+notebooks slice to its authoritative HF release.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Pretraining StackExchange Q&A component, published as the 2025 markdown processed corpus.

evidence.location: Pretraining section, three-stage data mix bullet
evidence.excerpt: Jupyter and Kaggle notebooks, GitHub issues, and StackExchange
evidence.explanation: Names StackExchange as a pretraining code/Q&A source.

evidence.excerpt: HuggingFaceTB/stackexchange_2025_md
evidence.explanation: Resolves StackExchange to its authoritative HuggingFaceTB markdown release.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Stage 2 math pretraining — MegaMath was added in the Stage 2 (8T → 10T) math slice along with FineMath4+ and InfiWebMath4+; SmolLM3 used Qwen Q&A, Pro synthetic rewrites, and text-code interleaved blocks subsets.

evidence.excerpt: Math: 10% - Introducing FineMath4+, InfiWebMath4+, and MegaMath (including Qwen Q&A, Pro synthetic rewrites, and text-code interleaved blocks)
evidence.explanation: Names MegaMath as the new Stage-2 math source and identifies the specific subsets used.

evidence.excerpt: LLM360/MegaMath
evidence.explanation: Resolves MegaMath to its authoritative LLM360 release.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Stage 2 code pretraining — Stack-Edu (125B-token educational-code subset of The Stack v2) was added when the code slice rose from 12% to 15%.

evidence.excerpt: Code: 15% - Adding Stack-Edu
evidence.explanation: Names Stack-Edu as the Stage-2 code addition.

evidence.excerpt: Stack-Edu is a 125B token dataset of educational code filtered from The Stack v2
evidence.explanation: Confirms the Stack-Edu identity and source.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Pretraining synthetic / educational corpus — smollm-corpus bundles Cosmopedia v2 (synthetic textbooks generated by Mixtral-8x7B-Instruct-v0.1), FineWeb-Edu-Dedup, and Python-Edu, used as part of the SmolLM3 pretraining mix.

evidence.excerpt: HuggingFaceTB/smollm-corpus
evidence.explanation: Lists smollm-corpus among the SmolLM3 pretraining datasets.

evidence.excerpt: Cosmopedia v2 is an enhanced version of Cosmopedia, the largest synthetic dataset for pre-training, consisting of over 39 million textbooks, blog posts, and stories generated by Mixtral-8x7B-Instruct-v0.1
evidence.explanation: Confirms Cosmopedia v2's generator (used to derive the generated_by edge to Mixtral).

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Pretraining mix component — Dolmino Mix 1124 (AllenAI's curated mix used in OLMo 2) was part of the SmolLM3 pretraining datasets collection.

evidence.excerpt: allenai/dolmino-mix-1124
evidence.explanation: Authoritative SmolLM3 pretraining dataset collection lists Dolmino Mix 1124.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Stage 3 reasoning/instruction pretraining — NVIDIA's OpenMathReasoning was introduced as part of the Stage 3 decay-mix reasoning data.

evidence.excerpt: introducing instruction and reasoning datasets such as OpenMathReasoning
evidence.explanation: Names OpenMathReasoning as a Stage-3 reasoning data source.

evidence.excerpt: nvidia/OpenMathReasoning
evidence.explanation: Resolves OpenMathReasoning to its authoritative NVIDIA HF release.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Stage-2/3 reasoning pretraining — NVIDIA's OpenCodeReasoning (R1-generated competitive-programming traces) appears in the SmolLM3 pretraining datasets collection.

evidence.excerpt: nvidia/OpenCodeReasoning
evidence.explanation: SmolLM3 pretraining collection lists OpenCodeReasoning as a constituent dataset.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Stage 3 (decay) reasoning pretraining — Meta's Natural Reasoning dataset entered the Stage-3 decay mix.

evidence.excerpt: facebook/natural_reasoning
evidence.explanation: SmolLM3 pretraining collection lists Natural Reasoning under Stage 3 (decay).

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Math pretraining substrate — InfiWebMath3+ (Stage 1) and InfiWebMath4+ (Stage 2) are filtered subsets of InfiMM-WebMath-40B (released by HuggingFaceTB under the FineMath repo using the FineMath classifier).

evidence.location: Pretraining section, Stages 1 and 2 math bullets
evidence.excerpt: Math: 3% - FineMath3+ and InfiWebMath3+ ... Math: 10% - Introducing FineMath4+, InfiWebMath4+, and MegaMath
evidence.explanation: Names InfiWebMath3+ and InfiWebMath4+ as math pretraining sources.

evidence.excerpt: We also release a filtered English text-only portion of the InfiMM-WebMath-40B dataset, classified using the same approach as FineMath
evidence.explanation: Identifies InfiMM-WebMath-40B as the source corpus that was filtered to produce the InfiWebMath3+/4+ subsets.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Mid-training reasoning data — OpenThoughts3-1.2M contributed to the 35B-token mid-training reasoning mix and also reappeared as both think and no_think SFT subsets in SmolTalk2.

evidence.excerpt: Our mid-training dataset contained 35B tokens sourced from Open Thought's OpenThoughts3-1.2M
evidence.explanation: Names OpenThoughts3-1.2M as a primary mid-training reasoning source.

evidence.excerpt: OpenThoughts3-1.2M: 1.14M rows
evidence.explanation: SmolTalk2's mid-training subset confirms inclusion of OpenThoughts3-1.2M.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Mid-training reasoning data — SmolLM3 used a subset of NVIDIA's Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset-v1.1, specifically the rows containing R1-generated reasoning traces.

evidence.excerpt: and a subset from NVIDIA's Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset-v1.1 with reasoning traces from R1
evidence.explanation: Confirms the dataset and the specific R1-traces subset used.

evidence.excerpt: Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset: 3.64M rows
evidence.explanation: SmolTalk2 mid-training subset confirms the row count used by SmolLM3.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Aggregator — SmolTalk2 is HuggingFaceTB's released bundle of all SmolLM3 mid-training, SFT (think + no_think), and preference subsets used in SmolLM3 post-training (~1.8B tokens SFT + APO preferences).

evidence.excerpt: We released all the datasets we used for training in [SmolTalk2]
evidence.explanation: SmolLM3 team confirms SmolTalk2 contains the post-training data used.

evidence.excerpt: Mid, SFT and Preference subsets
evidence.explanation: Defines SmolTalk2 as the aggregator of mid-training, SFT, and preference data for SmolLM3.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Post-training SFT source — original SmolTalk (with subsets including Smol-Magpie-Ultra, Smol-Constraints, Smol-Rewrite, Smol-Summarize, everyday-conversations, systemchats-30k) was used directly as no_think SFT data and as the prompt source for new Qwen3-32B-generated reasoning subsets in SmolTalk2.

evidence.excerpt: 244736 rows generated with Qwen3-32B with the prompts in SmolTalk ... SmolTalk (smollm3_smol-magpie-ultra): 406843 rows ... SmolTalk (smollm3_smol-rewrite): 53262 rows
evidence.explanation: SmolTalk subsets are reused directly and as prompt sources for SmolTalk2's reasoning data.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Preference (APO) post-training data — AllenAI's Tulu 3 8B Preference Mixture supplied the no_think preference pairs and (with Qwen3-32B/Qwen3-0.6B regenerating responses) the think preference pairs.

evidence.excerpt: a combination of the Tulu3 preference dataset for non-reasoning mode and new synthetic preference pairs for reasoning mode, that we generated from Qwen3-32B and Qwen3-0.6B
evidence.explanation: Names the Tulu3 preference dataset as the no_think preference source.

evidence.excerpt: Tulu 3 8B Preference Mixture (no_think): 231k rows ... Tulu 3 8B Preference Mixture (think): 216k rows where we generate the chosen responses with Qwen3-32B and the rejected responses with Qwen3-0.6B
evidence.explanation: Confirms both think and no_think preference subsets are derived from the Tulu 3 8B mixture.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Post-training SFT source — Tulu 3 SFT Personas IF was used directly (29,970 no_think rows) and as the prompt source for the new Multi-Turn IF subset (28,217 rows; turns generated with Qwen3-235B-A22B, responses with Qwen3-32B).

evidence.location: SFT Datasets (think + no_think) — Multi-Turn IF and Tulu 3 entries
evidence.excerpt: Tulu 3 SFT Personas IF: 29970 rows ... [NEW] Multi-Turn IF: 28217 rows ... We source prompts from Tulu 3 Personas IF, generate 2 verifiable turns using Qwen3-235B-A22B, and generate responses with Qwen3-32B in reasoning mode
evidence.explanation: Confirms both direct use and use as a prompt source for synthesized data.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Post-training SFT source — Cohere's Aya dataset prompts were used to generate 15,222 reasoning-mode SFT rows via Qwen3-32B for SmolTalk2.

evidence.excerpt: Aya: 15222 rows generated with Qwen3-32B with the prompts in Aya
evidence.explanation: Names Aya as the prompt source for a SmolTalk2 reasoning subset.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Post-training reasoning SFT source — s1K-1.1 (835 rows) was included directly in the SmolTalk2 reasoning SFT mix.

evidence.excerpt: s1k-1.1: 835 rows
evidence.explanation: Lists s1K-1.1 among think-mode SFT sources.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Post-training no_think SFT source — OpenHermes-2.5 contributed 384,900 no_think rows to SmolTalk2.

evidence.excerpt: OpenHermes-2.5: 384900 rows
evidence.explanation: Names OpenHermes-2.5 in the no_think SFT mix.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Post-training no_think SFT source — Hermes function-calling v1 contributed 8,961 rows to SmolTalk2 supporting tool-calling capabilities.

evidence.excerpt: hermes-function-calling-v1: 8961 rows
evidence.explanation: Names Hermes function-calling v1 in the no_think SFT mix.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Post-training no_think SFT source — Mixture of Thoughts (science split) contributed 86,110 rows to SmolTalk2 with reasoning traces removed.

evidence.location: SFT no_think Datasets — Mixture of Thoughts entry
evidence.excerpt: Mixture of Thoughts (science): 86110 rows where we remove the reasoning trace
evidence.explanation: Names Mixture of Thoughts as a no_think SFT source.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Long-context post-training SFT source — LongAlign was filtered to 64k-token samples, used directly (6,249 no_think rows) and as the prompt source for 7,526 Qwen3-32B-generated reasoning rows.

evidence.excerpt: LongAlign-64k-context-lang-annotated: 7526 rows generated with Qwen3-32B with the prompts in LongAlign-64k ... LongAlign-64k-context-lang-annotated (lang_6): 6249 examples. We filter LongAlign for samples up to 64k tokens
evidence.explanation: Confirms LongAlign serves as both direct SFT source and prompt source for synthesized reasoning data.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Primary post-training data generator — Qwen3-32B (a) generated reasoning-mode SFT data for SmolTalk2 think subsets (SmolTalk multilingual/everyday/systemchats: 244,736 rows each; LongAlign: 7,526; Aya: 15,222; Table-GPT: 13,201; Multi-Turn IF responses), (b) generated chosen completions for the 216k Tulu-3 think preference pairs, and (c) translated Smol-Magpie-Ultra and Smol-Constraints prompts into 8 languages.

evidence.excerpt: we generated synthetic data by prompting Qwen3-32B in reasoning mode with prompts from existing non-reasoning datasets
evidence.explanation: Names Qwen3-32B as the SFT reasoning data generator.

evidence.excerpt: generated with Qwen3-32B with the prompts in SmolTalk ... we generate the chosen responses with Qwen3-32B and the rejected responses with Qwen3-0.6B
evidence.explanation: Documents Qwen3-32B's role for both reasoning SFT generation and preference-pair chosen completions.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Preference rejected-response generator — Qwen3-0.6B was deliberately used to produce weaker rejected responses for the 216k Tulu-3 think preference pairs, paired against Qwen3-32B chosen responses for APO post-training.

evidence.excerpt: We selected generations from Qwen3-32B as 'chosen' and responses from Qwen3-0.6B as 'rejected'
evidence.explanation: Documents Qwen3-0.6B's role as the rejected-response generator in the preference mix.

evidence.excerpt: we generate the chosen responses with Qwen3-32B and the rejected responses with Qwen3-0.6B
evidence.explanation: Cross-confirms the Qwen3-0.6B rejected-generator role.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Generator of verifiable instruction-following turns — Qwen3-235B-A22B produced the two verifiable turns for the 28,217-row Multi-Turn IF SFT subset (responses then synthesized by Qwen3-32B in reasoning mode).

evidence.location: SFT think Datasets — Multi-Turn IF entry
evidence.excerpt: We source prompts from Tulu 3 Personas IF, generate 2 verifiable turns using Qwen3-235B-A22B, and generate responses with Qwen3-32B in reasoning mode
evidence.explanation: Identifies Qwen3-235B-A22B as the generator of verifiable IF turns used in SmolLM3's SFT mix.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Generator of agentic tool-calling traces — DeepSeek-V3-0324 produced the 9,079-row smolagents-toolcalling-traces SFT subset in SmolTalk2.

evidence.excerpt: [NEW] smolagents-toolcalling-traces: 9079 rows ... deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3-0324
evidence.explanation: Identifies DeepSeek-V3-0324 as the generator of the tool-calling-traces subset.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Pretraining synthetic-data generator (transitive via SmolLM-Corpus / Cosmopedia v2) — Mixtral-8x7B-Instruct-v0.1 generated the >39M synthetic textbooks, blog posts, and stories in Cosmopedia v2, which is a subset of the SmolLM-Corpus pretraining mix used in SmolLM3.

evidence.excerpt: HuggingFaceTB/smollm-corpus
evidence.explanation: Confirms smollm-corpus is part of SmolLM3's pretraining.

evidence.excerpt: Cosmopedia v2 is an enhanced version of Cosmopedia, the largest synthetic dataset for pre-training, consisting of over 39 million textbooks, blog posts, and stories generated by Mixtral-8x7B-Instruct-v0.1
evidence.explanation: Names Mixtral-8x7B-Instruct-v0.1 as the generator of Cosmopedia v2 content used in SmolLM-Corpus.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Post-training SFT data generator (transitive via SmolTalk / Smol-Magpie-Ultra) — Llama-3.1-405B-Instruct generated the 400K Smol-Magpie-Ultra SFT rows via the Magpie pipeline; Smol-Magpie-Ultra is a SmolTalk subset reused as 406,843 no_think SFT rows in SmolTalk2.

evidence.excerpt: SmolTalk (smollm3_smol-magpie-ultra): 406843 rows
evidence.explanation: Confirms Smol-Magpie-Ultra (a SmolTalk subset) is included in SmolLM3's no_think SFT data.

evidence.excerpt: Smol-Magpie-Ultra: the core component of our mix, consisting of 400K samples generated using the Magpie pipeline with /Llama-3.1-405B-Instruct
evidence.explanation: Identifies Llama-3.1-405B-Instruct as the generator behind Smol-Magpie-Ultra.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Reasoning-trace generator (transitive via OpenThoughts3-1.2M and OpenMathReasoning) — QwQ-32B generated 16x annotations per question for OpenThoughts3-1.2M (used in mid-training and SFT) and produced part of the chain-of-thought solutions in NVIDIA's OpenMathReasoning (used in Stage 3 pretraining).

evidence.excerpt: Annotate each question 16x with QwQ-32B, arriving at a 1.2M dataset
evidence.explanation: Documents QwQ-32B as the OpenThoughts3 annotator.

evidence.excerpt: We used Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct to preprocess problems, and DeepSeek-R1 and QwQ-32B to generate solutions
evidence.explanation: Documents QwQ-32B as one of two solution generators for OpenMathReasoning.

evidence.excerpt: Our mid-training dataset contained 35B tokens sourced from Open Thought's OpenThoughts3-1.2M ... introducing instruction and reasoning datasets such as OpenMathReasoning
evidence.explanation: Grounds back to SmolLM3's own documentation that both datasets are used.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Reasoning-trace generator (transitive via Llama-Nemotron R1 subset, OpenMathReasoning, and OpenCodeReasoning) — DeepSeek-R1 produced the R1-traces subset of the NVIDIA Llama-Nemotron mid-training data, the R1-generated solutions in OpenMathReasoning, and all output responses in OpenCodeReasoning.

evidence.excerpt: and a subset from NVIDIA's Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset-v1.1 with reasoning traces from R1
evidence.explanation: Direct subject-side citation that R1's reasoning traces fed SmolLM3's mid-training.

evidence.excerpt: DeepSeek-R1 and QwQ-32B to generate solutions
evidence.explanation: Confirms R1's role in OpenMathReasoning, which SmolLM3 uses in Stage 3.

evidence.excerpt: The output responses are generated by R1
evidence.explanation: Confirms R1 generated all OpenCodeReasoning outputs.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Pretraining-data preprocessor (transitive via OpenMathReasoning) — Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct preprocessed/refined the AoPS forum problems whose reasoning solutions form OpenMathReasoning, which entered SmolLM3 Stage 3 pretraining.

evidence.excerpt: Problem statement extracted from AoPS forums and refined with Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct ... We used Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct to preprocess problems
evidence.explanation: Identifies Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct as the AoPS-problem preprocessor for OpenMathReasoning.

evidence.excerpt: introducing instruction and reasoning datasets such as OpenMathReasoning
evidence.explanation: Grounds the chain back to SmolLM3's pretraining mix.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Math-pretraining filter source (transitive via FineMath classifier) — Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct produced the synthetic annotations used to train the FineMath educational-content classifier; that classifier filtered CommonCrawl into FineMath3+/4+ and InfiWebMath3+/4+, which entered SmolLM3 Stage 1 and Stage 2 math pretraining.

evidence.excerpt: we trained a mathematical content classifier using annotations generated by LLama-3.1-70B-Instruct
evidence.explanation: Identifies Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct as the annotation source training the FineMath classifier.

evidence.excerpt: Math: 3% - FineMath3+ and InfiWebMath3+ ... Math: 10% - Introducing FineMath4+, InfiWebMath4+
evidence.explanation: Grounds the chain back to SmolLM3's pretraining.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Code-pretraining filter source (transitive via Stack-Edu classifiers) — Llama3-70B-Instruct generated the synthetic annotations used to train the 15 language-specific StarEncoder-based classifiers that filtered The Stack v2 into the 125B-token Stack-Edu, used in SmolLM3 Stage 2 code pretraining.

evidence.excerpt: Trained 15 language-specific classifiers, using StarEncoder model on synthetic annotations generated by Llama3-70B-Instruct
evidence.explanation: Identifies Llama3-70B-Instruct as the source of annotations for the Stack-Edu classifiers.

evidence.excerpt: Code: 15% - Adding Stack-Edu
evidence.explanation: Grounds the chain back to SmolLM3's Stage-2 code mix.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Methodology — SmolLM3 borrowed OLMo 2's recipe of removing weight decay from embedding layers to improve training stability; embedding norms naturally stabilized at healthier values in SmolLM3 ablations.

evidence.excerpt: Following OLMo 2, we remove weight decay from embedding layers to improve training stability
evidence.explanation: Explicitly cites OLMo 2 as the methodological precedent for embedding-layer weight-decay removal.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Methodology — SmolLM3 borrowed Qwen2.5's approach of using YARN to extrapolate context beyond the trained window (64k → 128k inference).

evidence.excerpt: Following Qwen2.5, we use YARN to extrapolate beyond the training context length
evidence.explanation: Explicitly cites Qwen2.5 as the methodological precedent for the YARN context-length extrapolation recipe.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Base-model commonsense reasoning evaluation; also evaluated multilingually via MLMM HellaSwag.

evidence.excerpt: HellaSwag, ARC, BoolQ
evidence.explanation: Names HellaSwag in the base-model eval suite.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Base-model science/reasoning evaluation (reported as ARC and ARC-CF).

evidence.excerpt: HellaSwag, ARC, BoolQ
evidence.explanation: Names ARC in the base-model eval suite.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Base-model commonsense reasoning evaluation.

evidence.excerpt: HellaSwag, ARC, Winogrande, CommonsenseQA
evidence.explanation: Names Winogrande in the base-model eval suite.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Base-model commonsense Q&A evaluation.

evidence.excerpt: HellaSwag, ARC, Winogrande, CommonsenseQA
evidence.explanation: Names CommonsenseQA in the base-model eval suite.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Knowledge benchmark — reported as MMLU-CF (cloze-form) for the base model.

evidence.excerpt: MMLU-CF, MMLU Pro CF, PIQA
evidence.explanation: Names MMLU in the base-model eval suite.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Base-model knowledge evaluation (reported as MMLU Pro CF).

evidence.excerpt: MMLU-CF, MMLU Pro CF, PIQA
evidence.explanation: Names MMLU-Pro in the base-model eval suite.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Base-model physical commonsense evaluation.

evidence.excerpt: MMLU Pro CF, PIQA, OpenBookQA
evidence.explanation: Names PIQA in the base-model eval suite.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Base-model science Q&A evaluation.

evidence.excerpt: PIQA, OpenBookQA, GSM8K
evidence.explanation: Names OpenBookQA in the base-model eval suite.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Grade-school math reasoning evaluation for both base and instruct variants.

evidence.excerpt: GSM8K, MATH, HumanEval+
evidence.explanation: Names GSM8K in the base-model eval suite.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Competition-level math evaluation.

evidence.excerpt: GSM8K, MATH, HumanEval+
evidence.explanation: Names MATH in the base-model eval suite.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Used as the metric in the team's long-context data-mix ablations — they tested upsampling specific long-context data and found no further improvement on RULER, deciding that NoPE plus the natural decay-mix long samples were sufficient.

evidence.excerpt: During ablations, we found that upsampling specific long context data such as code repositories, books, and long web pages (beyond the naturally long samples in our mixture) didn't further boost performance on RULER and HELMET benchmarks
evidence.explanation: Identifies RULER as the ablation metric the team used to pick between data-mix options.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Used alongside RULER as the metric in the team's long-context data-mix ablations.

evidence.excerpt: didn't further boost performance on RULER and HELMET benchmarks
evidence.explanation: Identifies HELMET as a co-metric in the long-context data-mix ablation.

edges.subject: qwen/qwq-32b
edges.description: QwQ-32B (a depth-1 generator for SmolLM3 via OpenThoughts3 and OpenMathReasoning) was post-trained from the Qwen2.5-32B base via SFT and reinforcement learning.

evidence.excerpt: Base model: Qwen/Qwen2.5-32B
evidence.explanation: QwQ-32B's HF card explicitly names Qwen2.5-32B as its base model.

evidence.excerpt: QwQ is based on Qwen2.5
evidence.explanation: Reinforces the QwQ → Qwen2.5 base lineage.
